name: CRISPR Nuclease Selector
description: Recommends Cas9 or Cas12a for a CRISPR experiment. First compares valid guide counts (NGG for Cas9, TTTV for Cas12a); the nuclease with clearly more guides wins if the ratio meets margin_threshold (default 1.5x). When counts are within the margin, secondary criteria apply: multiplexing favors Cas12a, high specificity favors Cas12a, otherwise Cas9 is the default. AT-richness is reported as supporting context but does not override the primary signals.

inputs.name: seq
inputs.description: DNA sequence to analyze. Accepts a resource name (e.g. 'pBR322'), a raw sequence string, a FASTA-formatted string, or a GenBank-formatted string. The framework resolves the format automatically before passing a clean sequence to the function.

inputs.name: repair_template
inputs.description: True if a homology-directed repair (HDR) donor template will be provided; False for NHEJ-based knockout or indel. Noted in the rationale but does not change the nuclease recommendation -- both Cas9 and Cas12a support HDR and NHEJ.

inputs.name: num_targets
inputs.description: Number of distinct genomic loci to edit in one experiment. Values >= 2 indicate multiplexing. Defaults to 1. Multiplexing biases toward Cas12a because it processes crRNA arrays from a single transcript, simplifying multi-locus editing.

inputs.name: high_specificity
inputs.description: True if minimizing off-target edits is a priority (e.g. therapeutic context). When guide counts are within the margin, this biases the recommendation toward Cas12a, which is more mismatch-intolerant than wild-type SpCas9. Defaults to false.

inputs.name: cas12a_spacer_len
inputs.description: Length of the Cas12a spacer to evaluate for quality filtering. Must be 20-24. Defaults to 23 (LbCas12a standard). Adjust only when working with a Cas12a variant that uses a non-standard spacer length.

inputs.name: margin_threshold
inputs.description: Minimum ratio by which one nuclease must exceed the other's valid guide count for the count difference to drive the recommendation. Must be >= 1.0. Default 1.5 means one nuclease needs at least 50% more valid guides (e.g. 40 vs 36 is a tie; 40 vs 20 is a clear win). When counts fall within this margin, secondary criteria (multiplexing, specificity, default Cas9) decide.

outputs.description: A dictionary with ngg_count, tttv_count, recommendation ('Cas9' or 'Cas12a'), and rationale explaining the recommendation based on PAM density, repair strategy, and multiplexing needs.
